items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Core rendering matches the approved/reference translation and preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения». However, the line is duplicated in the actual translation (appears twice consecutively), which is not motivated by the source and harms timing/flow. Also, the reference note warns against translating as possession (“Shpak has...”), and the actual translation correctly avoids that by using noun phrases, so no issue there.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation:
Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
The Tsar, very pleased.
Hello, Tsar! Very pleased!
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: The first sentence block is identical to the reference and nails the comedic effect (banal Soviet politeness + drunken repetition). But the actual translation then adds extra repeated lines beyond the reference/source unit, effectively expanding the gag and risking subtitle bloat and rhythm distortion. If these extra lines correspond to adjacent Russian repetitions, they may be justified; but as evaluated strictly against this specific segment, the provided translation contains additional material not in the reference unit.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; preserves the sudden authoritarian, performative power tone. No issues.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the archaic/high-register melancholy and the comic mismatch with the modern setting. No issues.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; preserves the Tsar’s demonological framing and the subject role of “demons.” No issues.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the mock-biblical flourish and the religious punchline in an atheist-modern context. No issues.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; captures the MC/announcer vibe and the mass-invitation energy rather than a flat literal “Everyone dance.” No issues.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the breezy criminal satisfaction and idiomatic punch. No issues.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; crucially avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar,” preserving the joke’s clarity for non-Russian viewers. No issues.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; preserves the comic inventory cadence and the self-incriminating counting. No issues.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the calm, sing-song resignation and shared-absurdity humor. No issues.

summary.interpretation: 0.64 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.13 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
